channel.title: Yahoo News – World
channel.description: Latest world news from Yahoo News

item.title: Explosions heard in Iran, Mehr says Bandar Abbas situation under control
item.description: CAIRO, May 25 (Reuters) - Explosions were heard in Iran's Bandar Abbas city and coastal areas near the Strait of Hormuz, Iranian media reported on Monday, adding that the cause was unknown.
item.dc:creator: Reuters

item.title: Cuba publishes list of prisoners released in April amnesty
item.description: HAVANA, May 25 (Reuters) - Cuba on Monday published a complete list of prisoners released as part of an amnesty announced last month, at a time of tense negotiations between the island's government
item.dc:creator: Reuters

item.title: Israel to intensify Lebanon offensive to ‘crush’ Hezbollah
item.description: Prime Minister Netanyahu’s order comes despite ‘ceasefire’ agreed with Lebanon last month, which was recently extended.
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera

item.title: China Targets Trump’s Top Ally in Pacific
item.description: The pressure campaign against Japan shows economic statecraft is now a central tool in Beijing's arsenal, an analyst says.
item.dc:creator: Newsweek

item.title: Canada PM compares 'dangerous' Alberta separatist bid to Brexit
item.description: Canadian Prime Minister Mark Carney on Monday compared Alberta's plans to consider separating from Canada to Brexit, calling it "dangerous" and suggesting people may not appreciate the consequences of their vote.That is a very dangerous bluff," the prime minister said.
item.dc:creator: AFP

item.title: Israel steps up Lebanon strikes as Netanyahu escalates offensive
item.description: The Israeli army intensified strikes in southern Lebanon on Monday, as Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu said he had ordered the military to escalate its offensive in Lebanon in an effort to "crush" Hezbollah.The Israeli military also announced on Monday that a soldier had been killed the previous day in southern Lebanon.
item.dc:creator: AFP

item.title: A Palestinian woman and a young girl were killed in an Israeli airstrike in Gaza, officials say
item.description: DEIR AL-BALAH, Gaza Strip (AP) — A Palestinian woman and a young girl were killed in an Israeli airstrike in southern Gaza on Monday, hospital authorities said. The strike hit a tent sheltering a displaced family in the area of Muwasi, on the western side of Khan Younis, according to the Kuwait field hospital, which received the casualties. The fatalities were the latest among Palestinians in the coastal enclave since an October ceasefire deal attempted to halt a more than two-year war between Israel and the Palestinian militant Hamas group in Gaza.
item.dc:creator: AP

item.title: Pope Leo warns AI risks becoming tool of 'domination, exclusion and death' in new encyclical
item.description: The pope unveils "Magnifica Humanitas" encyclical, warning artificial intelligence risks becoming a tool of domination unless governments set moral limits.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Senegal president names economist Lo as prime minister
item.description: Source: Reuters

item.title: Spanish citizen evacuated from hantavirus-hit cruise ship tests positive
item.description: A Spanish national who was evacuated from the cruise ship MV Hondius and is isolating at a hospital in Madrid has tested positive of hantavirus, Spain’s Ministry of Health said Monday.

item.title: Meloni coalition wins Venice mayoral vote, defying polls
item.description: By Angelo Amante ROME, May 25 (Reuters) - Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni's right-wing coalition won the Venice mayoral election, projections showed on Monday, retaining control of the most prominent

item.title: Iran says obstacles remain, but acknowledges deal taking shape with U.S.
item.description: As the Trump administration signals hope for a possible deal, Iran says obstacles linger, and "frequent changes" in U.S. positions aren't helping.

item.title: Bolivian leader to slash own salary by 50% in gesture to protesters
item.description: Bolivian President Rodrigo Paz said Monday he would slash his salary and that of his ministers by 50 percent to try to end weeks of anti-government protests."This president has made the decision, as part of his efforts and commitment to the country, to reduce his salary by 50 percent," Paz announced in the southeastern city of Sucre.

item.title: Bolivia’s embattled president says will take a 50 percent salary cut
item.description: Paz says the salary cut for him and cabinet ministers shows the government’s ‘commitment’ to the country’.

item.title: Fire at Belgian town almost put out, smoke to fade during the night
item.description: BRUSSELS, May 25 (Reuters) - A fire at a textile warehouse in a town southwest of Brussels that prompted the evacuation of several streets on Monday was almost put out early in the evening and the

item.title: Spanish national evacuated from hantavirus cruise ship tests positive
item.description: MADRID, May 25 (Reuters) - A Spanish national in quarantine in a Madrid military hospital, who is among those evacuated from a cruise ship earlier this month, has tested positive of hantavirus,

item.title: Leftist icon, millionaire lawyer, conservative senator: Who will be Colombia's next leader?
item.description: A leftist figure allied with President Gustavo Petro, a right-wing millionaire lawyer who champions the tough-on-crime approach and a conservative opposition senator are the frontrunners in Colombia's presidential elections on Sunday.The philosopher, lawyer and reformist member of Colombia's main opposition party considers Uribe her "father."

item.title: Zelenskiy: Little progress in talks with U.S. on missile defences
item.description: May 25 (Reuters) - President Volodymyr Zelenskiy said on Monday that Ukraine had made little progress in talks with the United States on expanding production of missile defences and was working with

item.title: Israel escalates Gaza attacks as Netanyahu stalls ceasefire for polls
item.description: Analysts warn Netanyahu is exploiting a failing “ceasefire” to prolong the destruction of Gaza ahead of Israeli election

item.title: Tunisian court hands presidential critic Sonia Dahmani new jail term
item.description: Lawyer convicted for the second time this year as President Saied’s crackdown on dissent intensifies.

item.title: The rare Ebola outbreak is one danger. Attacks on healthcare workers are another
item.description: Every time Vanny Birungi, a volunteer with the Red Cross in eastern Congo, goes out to raise awareness about the latest Ebola outbreak as suspected cases near 1,000, she faces a double threat. One is the rare Bundibugyo type of Ebola, with no vaccine or treatment. The other is the anger and suspicion of residents who have pelted her with stones and verbal abuse in Bunia, a city at the heart of the outbreak.

item.title: Hasan Piker Says ‘They’re After Your Boy’ As Cuba Trip Investigated
item.description: The influencer referred to a federal probe into his visit to the Caribbean island.

item.title: Russian Orthodox cleric denies link to white substance found in car by Czech police
item.description: LONDON, May 25 (Reuters) - A former senior cleric in the Russian Orthodox Church said on Monday that Czech police had found small containers of a white substance in his car during a search a day

item.title: Migrants step up to support community in war-hit Beirut
item.description: At a busy Beirut centre, migrant volunteers stirred pots of okra soup and shaped balls of the African staple fufu, keeping food coming for those caught between the latest Israel-Hezbollah war.Across the city in a suburb of Beirut, kitchen shelves were stacked with sauces and tins crowded the walls as volunteers prepared green beans and fried rice balls.

item.title: Russia threatens more strikes on Kyiv, urges foreigners to leave city
item.description: Russia said Monday it plans to launch more strikes on Kyiv, including on its "decision-making centres", and repeated a call for foreign citizens and diplomats to leave the city.We are warning foreign citizens, including personnel of diplomatic missions and international organisations, to leave the city as soon as possible," it added.

item.title: Cambodia's new conscription law takes effect in wake of conflict with Thailand
item.description: Cambodia ’s new conscription law, which establishes penalties of up to five years in prison for those who evade military service, has come into effect, the country's Prime Minister Hun Manet said Monday. The law was signed on Saturday by Senate president Hun Sen in his capacity as acting head of state while King Norodom Sihamoni undergoes medical treatment for prostate cancer in China.

item.title: WHO urges DRCongo's neighbours to act immediately on Ebola risk
item.description: States neighbouring the Democratic Republic of Congo are at great danger from Ebola and should act immediately to counter the deadly virus, the head of the World Health Organisation said on Monday.One person is confirmed dead in neighbouring Uganda with a further six confirmed infected after Monday saw the health ministry confirm two new cases.

item.title: Carney says Alberta vote on separation could become 'dangerous bluff'
item.description: By David Ljunggren OTTAWA, May 25 (Reuters) - Canadian Prime Minister Mark Carney said on Monday that Alberta's planned vote on potentially breaking away from the rest of Canada, while not binding,

item.title: Iran hangs another protester as dozens more risk execution: rights groups
item.description: Iran on Monday executed a man on charges related to protests this year, the latest in a spree of hangings on political or security accusations since the start of the US-Israeli war with dozens more risking execution, rights groups said.Some three dozen men have been hanged on charges related to protests, membership of banned opposition groups, or espionage since wartime executions resumed in March, according to rights groups.

item.title: Train bomb in Pakistan’s Baloch region: Why violence is on the rise
item.description: Deadly blast in Pakistan marks uptick in Balochistan separatist violence.

item.title: Russia threatens strikes on Kyiv defence sites, urges foreigners to leave
item.description: KYIV, May 25 (Reuters) - Russia urged foreign nationals on Monday to leave Kyiv before it launches a "series of systematic strikes" against defence targets in Ukraine's capital, a day after one of its

item.title: Gunmen abduct 25 people in twin attacks in Nigeria's Kwara state, police say
item.description: By Camillus Eboh ABUJA, May 25 (Reuters) - Suspected bandits attacked a police station and a traditional ruler’s palace in Nigeria’s Kwara state early on Sunday, abducting at least 10 people and

item.title: Pope Leo calls for "disarming" of AI in technology-focused encyclical
item.description: In his "Magnifica Humanitas" encyclical, Pope Leo warns that as civilization grapples with the power of AI, the main challenge is remaining "profoundly human."

item.title: With AI manifesto, Leo joins pantheon of popes who urged world to change
item.description: By Joshua McElwee VATICAN CITY, May 25 (Reuters) - Catholic popes have been urging global leaders to address social justice issues for 135 years across some two dozen major documents that many of the

item.title: Turkey's pro-Kurdish party condemns ousting of main opposition
item.description: By Huseyin Hayatsever ANKARA, May 25 (Reuters) - Turkey's pro-Kurdish DEM Party condemned on Monday a court ruling that ousted the leadership of the main opposition CHP last week, calling a police

item.title: Land where notorious cartel leader was killed is being auctioned
item.description: The Mexican government announced the auction of a plot of land within the country club where drug kingpin "El Mencho" died during a clash with the army in February.

item.title: Australian Gaza aid flotilla activists allege abuse after Israeli abduction
item.description: Activists allege assaults and torture during Israeli detention after flotilla intercepted in international waters.

item.title: WHO chief says suspected Ebola deaths at 220 as epidemic ‘outpacing us’
item.description: Tedros ⁠Adhanom Ghebreyesus says a delay in detecting cases means responders were now ‘playing catch-up’.

item.title: S.Africa government holds urgent talks over anti-migrant tensions
item.description: South African government ministers held urgent talks Monday to address tensions over illegal migration as protesters again demanded that undocumented foreign nationals be made to leave the country.The government has urged African nations to address the economic and governance crises that cause people to flock to South Africa, the continent's most industrialised country.

item.title: Iran's top envoys discussing potential peace deal with Qatar prime minister, official says
item.description: Source: Reuters

item.title: Bolivia president Paz cuts salary by 50% as protests grip country
item.description: LA PAZ, May 25 (Reuters) - Bolivian President Rodrigo Paz said on Monday he will cut his salary by 50%.

item.title: Russian State TV Says Elections Not Going Trump’s Way, Trashes Marco Rubio
item.description: U.S. midterms “clearly won’t be in Trump’s favor,” a pundit told Russian state channel NTV.

item.title: Serbian president deepens ties with China while facing pressure from protests at home
item.description: Serbian President Aleksandar Vucic deepened ties with China on Monday during a trip to Beijing as he faced pressure at home from anti-government protests in his Balkan country, including a major rally that prompted clashes over the weekend. After a meeting with Chinese President Xi Jinping, the two leaders witnessed the signing of more than 20 cooperation agreements, covering areas such as politics, trade, technology and education, Chinese state broadcaster CCTV reported. “China supports Serbia in persisting on a development path that suits its own national conditions, and is willing to strengthen exchanges of governance experience with Serbia,” CCTV quoted Xi as saying.

item.title: Ebola patients flee in attacks on Congo health facilities, hobbling response
item.description: By Gradel Muyisa , Fiston Mahamba and Aaron Ross BUNIA, May 25 (Reuters) - Doctors operating on the front lines of the fight against Ebola in Congo, already grappling with shortages of basic supplies,

item.title: US Justice Department seeks to lift injunction on ballroom project after shooting
item.description: May 25 (Reuters) - The Justice Department has again asked a federal court to lift an injunction holding up progress on President Donald Trump's ballroom project, saying Saturday's shooting outside the

item.title: Former Scottish National Party chief pleads guilty to embezzling $540,000
item.description: By Alistair Smout LONDON, May 25 (Reuters) - Former Scottish National Party chief executive Peter Murrell pleaded guilty on Monday to embezzling more than £400,000 ($540,000), admitting diverting

item.title: Cambodia's Hun Sen pardons detained opposition leader
item.description: Cambodia's acting head of state, former prime minister Hun Sen, pardoned opposition leader Kem Sokha on Monday from a sentence of almost three decades for treason.Hun Sen, who ruled Cambodia for nearly four decades, stepped down as prime minister in 2023 and handed power to his eldest son, Hun Manet.

item.title: Israeli fire kills six-year-old girl and a woman in Gaza, medics say
item.description: CAIRO/GAZA, May 25 (Reuters) - An Israeli airstrike on a tent in the southern Gaza Strip on Monday killed two people including a six-year-old girl and wounded 17 other people, including children,

item.title: Locals at epicentre of DR Congo Ebola outbreak storm hospital
item.description: Locals at the epicentre of the Ebola outbreak in the DR Congo attacked the town hospital overnight, demanding the body of a religious leader who died from the virus, officials said on Monday."They wanted to retrieve the body of a Catholic shepherd who had died of Ebola," the official said on condition of anonymity.

item.title: Israeli opposition leader Lapid says Trump’s emerging deal with Iran is `bad for the region’
item.description: The deal being discussed between the U.S. and Iran fails to achieve any of Israel’s goals for the war, Israel’s opposition leader Yair Lapid said on Monday, as he accused Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu of failing to influence a better agreement. Lapid, who is part of an alliance attempting to unseat Netanyahu in elections this year, said details of the emerging deal are “disturbing.” “The deal is bad for Israel, bad for the region, bad for the citizens of Iran,” Lapid told reporters in Jerusalem.

item.title: Cambodian opposition leader Kem Sokha receives royal pardon for treason
item.description: Cambodian opposition leader Kem Sokha on Monday was granted a royal pardon from his 27-year sentence for treason, a month after an appeals court affirmed his conviction and punishment. Hun Sen, the Senate president acting as head of state in the absence of King Norodom Sihamoni, issued the pardon freeing Kem Sokha from house arrest. Sihamoni is in China on an extended stay for medical treatment.

item.title: WHO chief says fast-moving Ebola epidemic is outpacing response efforts
item.description: May 25 (Reuters) - The head of the World Health Organization said on Monday that the fast-moving Ebola outbreak in Democratic Republic of Congo and Uganda was outpacing response efforts, giving the

item.title: Everest operators must vet climbers better, says ascent record holder
item.description: By Gopal Sharma KATHMANDU, May 25 (Reuters) - Mount Everest expedition operators must focus on the experience and knowledge of climbers to lower risks and minimise deaths on the mountain, a British

item.title: Pakistan train bombing kills more than 30 people, official says
item.description: By Saleem Ahmed ISLAMABAD, May 25 (Reuters) - More than 30 people died in a suicide bombing of a train in southwestern Pakistan on Sunday, officials said on Monday, in the latest attack claimed by

item.title: Experts from Thai rescue join efforts to save 7 trapped in Laos cave
item.description: Seven Laotian villagers entered a cave five days ago searching for gold and were trapped inside after heavy rain triggered flash flooding.

item.title: Ebola death toll climbs in Congo as new infections confirmed in Uganda
item.description: The WHO chief says there have been 220 suspected deaths in the ongoing Ebola outbreak as Ugandan health authorities report two new Ebola infections.

item.title: Israeli right-wing ministers urge Netanyahu to resume Beirut strikes to counter Hezbollah drone attacks
item.description: By Steven Scheer and Maya Gebeily JERUSALEM/BEIRUT, May 25 (Reuters) - Israeli Finance Minister Bezalel Smotrich on Monday called on Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu to resume bombing Beirut in

item.title: Minibus crashes into elephant, killing 3 people in Uganda national park
item.description: Three people died and four others were injured after their vehicle collided with an elephant in Murchison Falls National Park, police said on Monday.

item.title: Possible Iran-US deal: What we know
item.description: The United States and Iran have both played down chances of a quick deal to end a war that has engulfed the Middle East and disrupted the global oil market.- A major issue in the talks is traffic through the Strait of Hormuz, the vital global conduit for oil shipments that has come under Iranian control since the outbreak of the war.

item.title: Cambodian king pardons former opposition leader
item.description: (Corrects to say decree was signed by Hun Sen, not Hun Manet, in paragraph 9) PHNOM PENH, May 25 (Reuters) - Cambodia's king has pardoned former opposition leader Kem Sokha for a treason ‌conviction,

item.title: China’s Xi praises ‘unbreakable’ Pakistan ties as Sharif visits Beijing
item.description: Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif says China and Pakistan are ‘iron brother’ countries.

item.title: Cattle market empties as fear grips Eid preparations in India’s West Bengal
item.description: Tensions rise in key state, won by BJP this month for first time, as political shift disrupts Eid al-Adha traditions.

item.title: Live updates: Iran downplays chances of immediate agreement with US
item.description: Iran says negotiators have reached a conclusion on many topics, but that doesn't mean "we're close to an agreement."

item.title: What to Know About Türkiye’s Deepening Democratic Crisis
item.description: Riot police forced into the headquarters of the country’s main opposition party.

item.title: Xi hails 'unbreakable' Pakistan ties, praises Iran peace efforts
item.description: By Liz Lee and Ethan Wang BEIJING, May 25 (Reuters) - President Xi Jinping hailed China's "unbreakable" friendship with Pakistan on Monday as he met Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif in Beijing, seeking

item.title: Russia says magnetic mines found on tanker at Ust-Luga port
item.description: MOSCOW, May 25 (Reuters) - Several NATO-produced magnetic mines had been detected on a tanker in Russia's Baltic Sea port of Ust-Luga, Russia's Investigative Committee said on Monday.

item.title: US and Iran Have Different Understandings of Peace Deal
item.description: “Do not believe the bluff of the defeated president,” Ebrahim Rezaei from Iran’s national security committee said Monday.

item.title: Over 20 taken to hospital after unknown substance allegedly sprayed in Tokyo shopping area
item.description: More than 20 people developed sore throats near a luxury department store in Tokyo’s posh Ginza shopping area on Monday after a person allegedly sprayed an unknown substance, Japanese fire department officials said. The Tokyo Fire Department said 26 people complained of suddenly developing throat pain and felt unwell near the Ginza Six shopping complex, and all but one of them were taken to a hospital. Fire department and police officials said an investigation was underway.

item.title: Explainer-What needs to be agreed to end the Iran war?
item.description: LONDON/DUBAI, May 25 - Iran's foreign ministry spokesperson said on Monday that conclusions have been reached on many topics discussed in a potential 14-point memorandum of understanding, but this

item.title: Rescuers race to reach 7 trapped in a Laos cave after flash flooding blocks the exit
item.description: Rescuers are racing against time to reach seven people who have been trapped in a cave in central Laos since last week. A group of villagers in the Xaisomboun province went into the cave to look for gold on May 19, but heavy rain triggered flash flooding that blocked the exit, according to Laos and Thai rescue teams involved in the operation. Bounkham Luanglath, who leads the Laos’ Rescue Volunteer for People, told the Associated Press on Monday that one of the people from the group escaped before the exit was blocked and alerted authorities.

item.title: Founder of viral Indian Gen Z account alleges crackdown, threats
item.description: By Saurabh Sharma and Aftab Ahmed May 25 (Reuters) New Delhi - A viral social media account focusing on the concerns of India’s Gen Z has come under attack, with its founder alleging hacking and

item.title: European Countries Condemn Israeli Minister Over Alleged Mistreatment of Flotilla Activists
item.description: E.U. lawmakers are set to discuss the possibility of sanctions on Itamar Ben-Gvir after flotilla activists reported abuse by Israeli forces.

item.title: Strait of Hormuz closure chokes trade and aid for Afghanistan
item.description: When landlocked Afghanistan and neighboring Pakistan started fighting late last year and their border crossings closed, Afghans turned to their western neighbor, Iran, for an alternate to Pakistan’s major shipping hub of Karachi. The port lies on the Strait of Hormuz, where war has stranded hundreds of ships and thousands of their crew. Meanwhile, thousands of Afghanistan-bound containers were stuck in Pakistan.

item.title: Spraying incident in luxury mall in Japan injures about 20
item.description: Two people were seen being taken on stretchers into ambulances after, authorities said, a man sprayed a substance inside an upscale mall in Tokyo.

item.title: Israel kills three in attacks on Lebanon, issues more displacement orders
item.description: Israel carries out attacks on multiple villages and towns in southern Lebanon.

item.title: Flooding kills at least nine in China's Chongqing, state media says
item.description: BEIJING, May 25 (Reuters) - The death toll from flooding in southwestern China's Chongqing has risen to nine and 11 people are missing following torrential rain in the municipality, state broadcaster

item.title: Pope Leo warns of AI fueling warfare in first major theological document
item.description: Pope Leo XIV says control of artificial intelligence must not remain in the hands “of a few” while warning that technology is fueling world conflicts, setting out his proposals in the first major theological document of his pontificate.

item.title: Greek police arrest 20 in Crete over suspected EU farm subsidies fraud
item.description: ATHENS, May 25 (Reuters) - Greek police arrested 20 people on the island of Crete on Monday, saying they had dismantled a criminal gang suspected of defrauding the European Union of agricultural

item.title: Iran warns deal with US not yet close, despite some progress
item.description: Iran warned Monday that, while some progress had been made, it was not yet close to striking a deal with the United States to end the Middle East war, after a weekend of mixed messages from Washington and Tehran.The war, which erupted after the United States and Israel attacked the Islamic republic on February 28 and saw Iran respond with missile and drone attacks across the region, drove energy prices higher.

item.title: Senegal's parliament speaker quits two days after prime minister sacked
item.description: DAKAR, May 25 (Reuters) - Senegal's parliament speaker, El Malick Ndiaye, has announced his resignation, deepening political turmoil in the West African nation two days after the president dismissed

item.title: Iranians find 'peace and safety' in Mecca during hajj
item.description: Being in Mecca has been nothing short of blissful for Hassan Qadiri, where participating in the festivities of the hajj pilgrimage is a welcome relief after weeks of devastating war back home in Iran.The beginning of the hajj this week comes as speculation was rife over a potential agreement between Washington and Tehran.

item.title: India orders migrant detention centres sparking explusion fears
item.description: India's ruling Hindu-nationalist party has ordered detention centres for undocumented Bangladeshis and Rohingyas in West Bengal state, sparking fear among minorities that it could lead to arbitrary expulsions.The party has pursued similar policies in the neighbouring state of Assam, where it has overseen sweeping identification drives and largescale detentions.

item.title: Iran says conclusions reached on many topics in potential U.S. memorandum but no deal imminent
item.description: May 25 (Reuters) - Iran's foreign ministry spokesperson said on Monday that conclusions have been reached on many topics discussed in a potential memorandum of understanding with the U.S., but this

item.title: Rubio says US will find ‘another way’ if Iran talks fail
item.description: US secretary of state says a ‘pretty solid’ deal is on the table in terms of opening up the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: What we know and don't know about the emerging deal to end the Iran war
item.description: A deal appears to be emerging between the United States and Iran to end the war and open the Strait of Hormuz, and U.S. President Donald Trump over the weekend said it had been “largely negotiated.” Trump spoke after calls with allies in the Middle East, including a separate call with Israel. Details come from two regional officials and a U.S. official who spoke on condition of anonymity to discuss the sensitive negotiations.

item.title: Indian sailors risk work at sea, as Iran war grinds on
item.description: Born to landless Indian farmers, Sunil Pooniya thought a job at sea would be his ticket out of poverty, instead his first voyage saw him diving into the ocean to escape a deadly attack driven by the Iran war.The attack on Pooniya's ship killed two fellow Indians -- the country's sailors are among the highest merchant maritime casualties from the Middle East war.

item.title: At least 82 killed after massive gas explosion rips through coal mine in China
item.description: A massive gas explosion ripped through a coal mine in China's Shanxi province, killing at least 82 people and hospitalizing 120 others, the AP reports.

item.title: Minibus strikes a parked vehicle on a Pakistan highway, killing 17 people
item.description: Source: AP

item.title: Pilgrims kick off hajj as war's trajectory hangs in the balance
item.description: Over 1.5 million Muslims began filling a vast tent city in the holy city of Mecca on Monday for an annual hajj pilgrimage carried out against the backdrop of hopes for an end to the war in the Middle East.Despite the heat and the uncertainty over the war, pilgrims in Mecca were ecstatic.

item.title: Qalibaf reelected as Iran's parliament speaker, Fars says
item.description: Source: Reuters

item.title: As Iran diplomacy picks up, Rubio tours Taj Mahal
item.description: As diplomacy intensifies on ending the Iran war, top US diplomat Marco Rubio was spending Monday not in negotiations but at India's world-famous monument to love, the Taj Mahal."It's one of the wonders of the world," Rubio said of the Taj Mahal.

item.title: 3 dead, 17 mostly workers still missing in collapse of unfinished hotel in Philippines
item.description: Rescuers pulled out two workers early Monday from an immense pile of rubble that was a nine-story hotel which collapsed while under construction in a northern Philippine city, bringing the death toll to three with 17 others still missing, officials said. One of the workers emerged dead while emergency personnel struggled in the early morning hours to revive the other one in an ambulance near the pile of concrete slabs, twisted iron bars and aluminum scaffoldings that was all that remained of the building in Angeles City of Pampanga Province. The poignant scene was witnessed by a small group of journalists, including from The Associated Press, who stood by as hundreds of rescuers led by firefighters and police scrambled for hours to extricate the two workers, who were at the time alive but trapped under concrete slabs and iron bars.

item.title: Global tensions set to stalk Singapore's flagship defence summit
item.description: (Corrects name of political analyst in seventh paragraph) SINGAPORE/HONG KONG, May 25 (Reuters) - War in Iran, strained U. commitments in Asia and rising tensions over Taiwan will likely dominate the agenda at this week's annual Shangri-La Dialogue, the region's premier defence ‌forum.

item.title: Rubio's visit to India focuses on US trade tensions, the Quad alliance and sightseeing
item.description: U.S. Secretary of State Marco Rubio’s visit to India marks an effort by Washington and New Delhi to steady strained relations as the two countries seek to reinforce strategic and economic ties despite recent trade tensions. In meetings Sunday with Indian Prime Minister Narendra Modi and Foreign Minister Subrahmanyam Jaishankar, Rubio stressed cooperation on trade, energy, defense and maritime security. Rubio said the countries remained strategically aligned and expressed optimism about a broader trade deal.

item.title: Australian spy boss says he shifted resources from counterterrorism before Hanukkah attack
item.description: An Australian spy agency boss told an inquiry on Monday he had pivoted resources away from counterterrorism to espionage and foreign interference investigations a few years before two gunmen massacred 15 people at a Sydney Hanukkah celebration. Mike Burgess, director-general of the Australian Security Intelligence Organization, the nation’s main domestic spy agency known as ASIO, was testifying at a wide-ranging government inquiry into the spread of antisemitism in Australia ahead of the attack at Bondi Beach on Dec. 14.

item.title: Australia's spy chief says antisemitism was left unchecked after Gaza war
item.description: By Christine Chen SYDNEY, May 25 (Reuters) - Antisemitism in Australia was left unchecked after the October 2023 outbreak of the Israel-Gaza war, fuelling violence against Jewish people, the country's

item.title: Iran executes man involved in January protests, state media reports
item.description: DUBAI, May 25 (Reuters) - Iran executed a man over charges related to the anti-government protests that took place nationwide in January, state media reported on Monday.

item.title: Pakistan Shi'ites deported from UAE return to lost jobs, frozen savings
item.description: By Saad Sayeed and Mubasher Bukhari CHAKWAL, Pakistan, May 25 (Reuters) - In a cluster of villages in Pakistan's largely rural Chakwal district, more than 100 Shi'ite Muslims have returned from the

item.title: Muslims begin the annual Hajj in sweltering heat against a backdrop of war concerns
item.description: The annual Hajj pilgrimage, one of the Five Pillars of Islam, officially began Monday. More than 1.5 million pilgrims have arrived in Saudi Arabia from outside the country, Saleh bin Saad Al-Murabba, commander of the Hajj passport forces, said Friday. The faithful have been pouring into the country for the Hajj against the backdrop of a tenuous ceasefire in the Iran war and related regional tensions and uncertainty.

item.title: Rubio says US will either have a good agreement with Iran or deal with it 'another way'
item.description: May 25 (Reuters) - Secretary of State Marco Rubio said on Monday the U.S. will either have a good agreement with Iran or deal with the country "another way."

item.title: Philippine construction collapse toll hits four, over dozen missing
item.description: The death toll rose to four Monday in the collapse of a building under construction near the Philippine capital, with more than a dozen people still believed missing, authorities said.Due to the uncertainty, authorities said approximately 17 other people were still considered missing, mostly construction workers who were sleeping at the building site when disaster struck.

item.title: Asian Politics Headlines at 5:48 a.m. GMT
item.description: Source: AP

item.title: Africa Day 2026: Has the continent achieved true liberation?
item.description: Africa Day 2026 spotlights power, debt and digital control as Africans redefine sovereignty.

item.title: Deadly suicide blast rips through Pakistan train route, killing at least 23
item.description: At least 23 killed and 70 wounded after a suicide bomber reportedly detonated an explosives-laden vehicle alongside an incoming passenger train in Quetta, Pakistan.

item.title: Truck overturns in central Bangladesh, killing at least 15 people
item.description: A truck carrying iron rods and extra passengers overturned on a major highway early Monday in central Bangladesh, leaving at least 15 people dead and 10 others injured, police said. The crash happened after the driver lost control around 5 a.m. at Soratoil area in Tangail district, 83 kilometers (52 miles) northwest of the nation's capital, Dhaka, said local police chief Fuad Hossain. Hossain said the truck was carrying hitchhiking passengers and travelling from Dhaka to the northern region as vacationers began traveling ahead of the Islamic festival Eid al-Adha on Thursday.

item.title: Australian activists return after Gaza flotilla incident, allege Israeli abuse
item.description: By Stefica Bikesh and Cordelia Hsu SYDNEY, May 25 (Reuters) - Australian activists detained by Israel while on a flotilla attempting to deliver aid to Gaza have returned home, with organisers alleging

item.title: Toshifumi Suzuki, 'father' of Japan convenience stores, dies at 93
item.description: Former chairman of Seven & i Holdings Toshifumi Suzuki, credited for the global success of 7-Eleven convenience stores, has died at the age of 93, the company said Monday.Suzuki is known for opening the first 7-Eleven store in Japan in 1974 and growing the business into the world's largest convenience store chain, including through turning the struggling US headquarters into a subsidiary of the Japanese company and rebuilding it.

item.title: Thai beer heir sexual abuse allegations ignite rare public reckoning
item.description: A high-profile alleged sexual abuse case within a wealthy Thai beer brewing family has prompted a wave of painful accounts from survivors of unconnected abuse in the conservative country.Experts said the case marks an unprecedented shift in Thailand -- which never had a #MeToo movement -- with Thais more openly discussing sexual harassment and abuse.

item.title: Philippine construction collapse toll hits three, 17 missing
item.description: The death toll rose to three Monday in the collapse of a building under construction near the Philippine capital, with 17 people still believed missing, authorities said.Seventeen other people, mostly construction workers who were sleeping at the building site when disaster struck, remained missing, Sajili said.

item.title: Pope to release major artificial intelligence manifesto
item.description: Pope Leo XIV will release on Monday his long-awaited manifesto on artificial intelligence (AI), a bid to address ethical and social challenges as the technology rapidly develops worldwide.The release of "Magnificent Humanity" follows several years of study by the Church of AI-related technologies.

item.title: With ice cream and giant fans, hajj pilgrims battle searing heat
item.description: To avoid the punishing sun, Inas Gamal abandoned her ambitious plan of spending the days ahead of the hajj praying in Mecca's Grand Mosque and retreated to the comfort of an air-conditioned hotel room to perform her daytime prayers."I had planned to perform all my prayers at the Grand Mosque, but I couldn't go down for the prayers held during the day."

item.title: Death toll rises to three with 17 still missing after Philippine building collapse
item.description: Source: Reuters

item.title: Three people killed, one injured in accident at Petronas floating vessel off Malaysia
item.description: KUALA LUMPUR, May 25 (Reuters) - Three people were killed and one was injured in an accident during lifeboat maintenance work at a floating storage and offloading (FSO) vessel operated by Malaysian

item.title: Photos of a shooting near the White House in Washington
item.description: Source: AP

item.title: Mass tanker blackout rattles Gulf ahead of 1.35M-barrel oil transfer amid US-Iran talks: firm
item.description: Maritime tracking signals collapse near the UAE's Fujairah oil hub hours before President Trump announced progress on a bilateral peace deal with Iran, according to an AI maritime firm.

item.title: Ecuador’s Noboa pledges to extradite criminals in State of the Union speech
item.description: The right-wing president highlighted anti-crime operations and economic progress, while critics warned of abuses.

item.title: Cuba thanks China for rice shipment amid worsening humanitarian conditions
item.description: The Caribbean island has faced a fuel blockade that has cut off essential supplies, as Trump seeks regime change.

item.title: Senegal parliament speaker steps down in political crisis
item.description: The speaker of Senegal's parliament said Sunday he was resigning, two days after his close ally was fired as prime minister, deepening a political crisis in the debt-laden West African country.Faye has to name another prime minister to replace Sonko -- and that choice will have to be approved by parliament within three months of his nomination, said Ndiaye.

item.title: Deadly Israeli strikes pound south, east Lebanon
item.description: Israeli strikes pounded south and east Lebanon Sunday despite a ceasefire as Hezbollah's chief expressed hope for an agreement between Iran and the United States to end the Middle East war that includes Lebanon.Hezbollah drew Lebanon into the Middle East war on March 2 with rocket fire at Israel in retaliation for the killing of Iran's supreme leader in US-Israeli strikes.

item.title: More than 900 suspected Ebola cases identified in DRC, WHO chief says
item.description: May 24 (Reuters) - More than 900 suspected Ebola cases, including 101 confirmed cases, have been identified in the Democratic Republic of Congo, WHO Chief Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus said on Sunday.

item.title: Senegal parliament speaker steps down as political crisis worsens
item.description: The move by speaker El Malick Ndiaye clears the way for sacked premier Ousmane Sonko to run for head of parliament.

item.title: Rubio accuses Hezbollah of trying to 'drag Lebanon back into chaos'
item.description: US Secretary of State Marco Rubio accused Hezbollah in a statement Sunday of trying to plunge Lebanon "back into chaos."Rubio denounced what he called Hezbollah's "reckless call to overthrow Lebanon's democratically elected government" and said the pro-Iran armed group was "actively trying to drag Lebanon back into chaos and destruction."

item.title: Ukraine’s battlefield is transforming the future of NATO
item.description: NATO allies increasingly study Ukraine's wartime innovations in drones and defense production as the alliance prepares for its July summit in Ankara.

item.title: Possible fissure in California chemical tank may help prevent explosion, fire official says
item.description: Source: Reuters

item.title: Israeli strikes kill six in southern Lebanon amid fresh evacuation orders
item.description: Israel’s attacks in southern Lebanon are expanding despite supposed ceasefire.

item.title: Turkey riot police use tear gas to take opposition party HQ
item.description: Hundreds of Turkish riot police firing teargas forced their way into the Ankara headquarters of the country's main opposition party on Sunday, days after a court had dismissed its leadership, AFP journalists saw."They stormed our headquarters, used tear gas, beat us with batons, ransacked the party (building) and threw us out," Ozel told AFP on Sunday evening.

item.title: Shark kills spearfisher in front of friends: "Terrifying thing to see"
item.description: A spearfisher was killed on the Great Barrier Reef on Sunday in Australia's second fatal shark attack in just over a week, police said.

item.title: Nigeria's Tinubu to run for second term after party primary win
item.description: LAGOS, May 24 (Reuters) - Nigeria's President Bola Tinubu will seek a second and final four-year term in January after overwhelmingly defeating a little-known challenger in ruling party primaries,

item.title: Forensic experts sift through ruined dormitory in Russian-held Luhansk region
item.description: STAROBILSK, Russian-controlled Ukraine, May 24 (Reuters) - - Forensic experts on Sunday sifted through the ruins of a dormitory largely destroyed in what Russian authorities say was a Ukrainian drone

item.title: Possible deal on Iran divides US lawmakers largely along party lines
item.description: May 24 (Reuters) - U.S. lawmakers appearing on Sunday morning talk shows split sharply over a potential deal to end the Iran war, with Republicans mostly backing the publicly reported contours of an

item.title: What’s in the proposed deal that could end the US-Iran conflict?
item.description: Iran and the United States have signaled they are closing in on an agreement to turn the existing ceasefire that ended weeks of conflict into a more long-lasting settlement.

item.title: Zelensky Calls For 'Consequences' After Russia Hits Kyiv With One of Heaviest Bombardments Since War Began
item.description: The attack included the launch of an advanced ballistic Oreshnik missile, which is capable of carrying nuclear warheads.

item.title: Iran recounts historic battles in response to Trump’s talk of agreement
item.description: Iran cites battles from history in response to Trump’s latest comments on an imminent deal between the US and Iran.

item.title: Turkey riot police use tear gas to take opposition party HQ
item.description: Hundreds of Turkish riot police using teargas forced their way into the Ankara headquarters of the main CHP opposition party on Sunday after a court dismissed its leadership, AFP journalists saw.A prominent rights NGO, Human Rights Watch, on Saturday warned that President Recep Tayyip Erdogan's government was undermining Turkish democracy with "abusive tactics" against the CHP. Last year, Turkish authorities jailed Erdogan's main political rival, Istanbul mayor Ekrem Imamoglu, who was the CHP's

item.title: 2 dead, dozens wounded after Russia launches large attack on Ukraine
item.description: Russia used a powerful hypersonic missile that has nuclear capabilities to carry out a massive attack Sunday in Kyiv, Ukraine.

item.title: Three months of classes held online in Iran because of the war
item.description: Every afternoon since Iran's war with the United States and Israel erupted, Sarah has sat with her seven-year-old son for online classes.Neither the United States nor Israel officially claimed responsibility, but a New York Times investigation concluded that a US Tomahawk cruise missile hit the elementary school.

item.title: Rescuers search for 20 missing after Philippine building collapse
item.description: Rescuers were racing on Sunday to free two workers pinned under a building that collapsed in the northern Philippines, killing a Malaysian man and leaving 20 people missing.The man, from Malaysia's northern Sabah state, had managed to communicate with rescuers by phone while pinned under a hotel wall shortly after the collapse at 3:00 am Sunday (1900 GMT Saturday), acting Philippine fire chief Rico Kwan Tiu told AFP. "Unfortunately, when we retrieved him just now, he was already lifeless," he ad

item.title: Bahrain jails nine defendants for life for ‘cooperating’ with Iran’s IRGC
item.description: Convictions come amid an intensified crackdown by Bahraini authorities on individuals accused of having ties to Tehran.

item.title: France bans Ben-Gvir: Which other Israeli leaders have been penalised?
item.description: France bans the Israeli far-right minister after his ‘unspeakable’ behaviour against arrested Gaza flotilla activists.

item.title: For third year in a row, Israel blocks Hajj pilgrimage for Gaza Muslims
item.description: As millions of Muslims gather in Mecca, Palestinians in Gaza face a third year of heartbreak as Israel has shut border.

item.title: Yemen family deprived of aid reduced to eating tree leaves
item.description: With no food aid, 65-year-old Saeedah Mohammed heads out with a plastic bag to pick tree leaves near her displacement camp in southern Yemen, before serving them to her grandchildren to stave off hunger.So now she gathers leaves and slips them into her plastic bag.

item.title: 24 killed, dozens more injured in suicide bombing in Pakistan
item.description: Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif called the attack a "cowardly act of terrorism" on social media.

item.title: Possible Iran-US deal: What we know
item.description: The United States and Iran appear closer than ever to a deal that would end a war that has engulfed the Middle East and disrupted the global oil market.- A key sticking point in the talks is traffic through the Strait of Hormuz, a vital global conduit for oil shipments that has come under Iranian control since the outbreak of the war.

item.title: Potential 60-day ceasefire on table with Iran deal
item.description: “Final aspects and details of the deal are currently being discussed and will be announced shortly," Trump said.

item.title: Guide Kenton Cool scales Everest for the 20th time and says not ready to quit yet
item.description: One of the most accomplished mountain guides on Mount Everest is not ready to hang up his boots. Briton Kenton Cool this week scaled the peak for the 20th time, breaking his own record for the most ascents of the world’s highest mountain by a non-Sherpa guide. The 52-year-old from southwest England had said last year that he would be only climbing Everest maybe one more time before switching to other smaller peaks.

item.title: Montreal sex workers strike during Canada Grand Prix
item.description: Dozens of sex workers in Montreal went on strike during the Canadian Formula 1 Grand Prix, demanding better working conditions and the decriminalization of sex work, protesters said.They said these fees are often increased for events like the Grand Prix, forcing dancers to pay up to 150 Canadian dollars ($108) to work.

item.title: Bomb attack near railway station in southwest Pakistan kills at least 23
item.description: A suicide bomber detonated an explosives-laden vehicle near a railway track as a passenger train passed through the southwestern Pakistani city of Quetta on Sunday, killing at least 19 people and wounding over 70 others, officials said.

item.title: Syria holds legislative elections in Kurdish-majority northeast
item.description: Syria held follow-up parliamentary elections on Sunday in the Kurdish-majority northeastern province of Hassakeh and the town of Kobani, areas that were reintegrated under government control after fighting in January. Other parts of Syria voted for the People's Assembly last October, almost a year after the Assad dynasty was ousted in a rebel offensive.

item.title: Turkish riot police enter opposition headquarters to evict ousted leaders
item.description: ANKARA, May 24 (Reuters) - Turkish riot police fired tear gas and broke through the gates of the main opposition party's headquarters to evict its ousted leadership on Sunday, a Reuters witness said.

item.title: Dread and denial at heart of deadly DR Congo Ebola outbreak
item.description: Unlike other residents of Mongbwalu, a town at the heart of the eastern Democratic Republic of Congo's latest devastating Ebola outbreak, Laureine Sakiya believes that the blood-letting virus exists after seeing some of her neighbours die.Some believed that the latest epidemic was of a "mystical malady", a common belief in some remote areas of the DRC. "At the beginning, people believed it was a coffin affair," said Jonathan Imbalapay, a civil society leader in Mongbwalu.

item.title: Details emerge of a potential Iran deal as US cites progress
item.description: The United States is close to reaching a deal with Iran that would end the war, reopen the Strait of Hormuz and see Iran give up its stockpile of highly enriched uranium, with the details and timelines to be worked out later, regional officials told The Associated Press on Sunday. Iran has not publicly committed to giving up its uranium — a key demand of U.S. President Donald Trump — and the sides have seemed close to a deal at different points in recent weeks without clinching one. Secretary of State Marco Rubio, on a visit to India, said Sunday that “significant progress, although not final progress, has been made” in the negotiations, and that the world would no longer need to fear Iran getting a nuclear weapon, without elaborating.

item.title: Shark kills man in Australia's Queensland state
item.description: A man died after a shark attack in northern Queensland state on Sunday, the third fatal shark attack this year in Australia.Sunday's fatal shark attack is the third in Australia this year, and comes a week after a man was killed by a shark while spearfishing in Western Australia.

item.title: Israeli attack on Gaza kills three family members, including infant
item.description: Mohammad Abu Mallouh, Alaa Zaqlan and their child, Osama, killed in the attack on Nuseirat refugee camp, medics say.

item.title: Bahrain court jails nine for life for collaborating with Iran's Revolutionary Guards
item.description: May 24 (Reuters) - A Bahraini court sentenced nine defendants to life in prison and two others to three years in jail for collaborating with Iran's Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) to carry out what

item.title: Second Ebola treatment center set on fire in epicenter of disease's outbreak
item.description: Angry residents of a town at the epicenter of the Ebola outbreak in eastern Congo​ attacked and burned a tent that was part of a health center where people are being treated for the virus.

item.title: Russia hits Ukraine with Oreshnik missile in one of war's biggest attacks on Kyiv
item.description: By Yuliia Dysa and Daniel Flynn KYIV, May 24 (Reuters) - Russia pounded Kyiv and surrounding areas with hundreds of drones and missiles on Sunday in one of the heaviest bombardments of the city since

item.title: India voices concern on US visas but sees alignment with Rubio
item.description: India voiced concern on Sunday over a US visa crackdown, striking a rare critical note even as it expressed broad alignment with Secretary of State Marco Rubio on other fractious issues.Jaishankar said he "apprised Secretary Rubio of challenges that legitimate travellers face in respect of visa issuance".

item.title: Turkey orders police to evict ousted opposition leadership from headquarters
item.description: ANKARA, May 24 (Reuters) - Turkish authorities ordered police to evict the leadership of the main opposition Republican People's Party (CHP) from their headquarters on Sunday, enforcing a court ruling

item.title: The Subtle Show of Force Behind Trump’s Softer China Tones
item.description: President Donald Trump is personally fronting America's new approach to China.

item.title: Spearfisher mauled on Great Barrier Reef in Australia's second fatal shark attack in a week
item.description: A spearfisher was mauled on the Great Barrier Reef on Sunday in Australia’s second fatal shark attack in just over a week, police said. The 39-year-old man was with three friends diving from a boat at Kennedy Shoal off the Queensland state coast south of Cairns when he was attacked, Police Inspector Elaine Burns said. The victim, a Cairns resident, was brought by boat around noon to the tourist town of Hull Heads where paramedics were waiting.

item.title: Pakistan train blast kills at least 24 in Balochistan
item.description: A blast targeting a train carrying military personnel killed at least 24 people on Sunday in Pakistan's turbulent southwestern province of Balochistan, a senior official said.The local official told AFP that the train carrying army personnel and their family members was going from Quetta to Peshawar in Pakistan's northwest.

item.title: At least 24 killed in Pakistan train blast claimed by separatist militants
item.description: (Refiles to remove duplication in paragraphs 2, 3) By Saleem Ahmed May 24 (Reuters) - A bomb blast hit a shuttle train carrying Pakistani security personnel and their families in the southwestern

item.description: (Refiles to remove duplication in paragraphs 2, 3) May 24 (Reuters) - A bomb blast hit a shuttle train carrying Pakistani security personnel and their families in the ‌southwestern province of Balochistan on Sunday, officials said, in the latest major attack claimed ‌by separatist militants. The explosion killed at least 24 people and injured around 70, according to three provincial government and security ​officials, who spoke on the condition of anonymity as they are not authorized to speak t

item.description: The proposed US-Iran peace deal seeks to reopen the strategically vital Strait of Hormuz amid lingering disagreements.

item.title: Israeli fire kills parents and their infant in Gaza, medics say
item.description: CAIRO/GAZA, May 24 (Reuters) - An Israeli airstrike on an apartment in a refugee camp in central Gaza on Sunday left three people dead, including a six-month-old child, health officials said.

item.title: Yemen war sees scramble for scant resources between displaced and locals
item.description: Reduced aid to Yemen and a domestic economic crisis see extreme suffering among the displaced at the Maryamah camp.

item.description: MOSCOW, May 24 (Reuters) - Russian forces hit targets in Ukraine using four types of missiles - Oreshnik, Iskander, Kinzhal and Zircon - in overnight attacks on Sunday, in retaliation for Kyiv's

item.title: Cyprus votes for new parliament with corruption and living costs in focus
item.description: By Michele Kambas NICOSIA, May 24 (Reuters) - Cypriots went to the polls on Sunday in a parliamentary election expected to deliver gains for anti-corruption campaigners and the far right, while

item.title: Suicide car bomb attack on train in Pakistan kills at least 24
item.description: The Balochistan Liberation Army has claimed responsibility for the attack.

item.title: Three dead and 17 missing after flooding in China's Chongqing
item.description: BEIJING, May 24 (Reuters) - The death toll has risen to three with 17 missing following flooding in the Chongqing municipality of southwestern China, China's state-run Xinhua reported.

item.title: Hoax fire call hits flat of Polish president’s family member, Tusk says
item.description: WARSAW, May 24 (Reuters) - Poland is facing a series of false alarms about fires or other threats, including one on Saturday at a flat in Gdansk belonging to a member of President Karol Nawrocki's

item.title: A suicide bombing near a railway track in southwest Pakistan kills at least 23 people
item.description: A suicide bomber detonated an explosives-laden vehicle near a railway track as a passenger train passed through the southwestern Pakistani city of Quetta on Sunday, killing at least 23 people and wounding over 70 others, officials said. The attack happened in an area where security forces are usually stationed, badly damaging several nearby buildings and smashing more than a dozen vehicles parked along the road, according to witnesses and images circulating on social media. Doctors at local hospitals said they had received the wounded, with 20 in critical condition.

item.title: Russia kills 4 in massive Ukraine attack after vowing retaliation
item.description: Russia pounded Kyiv with a massive missile and drone attack that killed four people early on Sunday, authorities said, after President Vladimir Putin threatened retaliation for strikes in Russian-occupied eastern Ukraine.Ukrainian authorities and the US embassy had earlier warned of a possible significant attack on Kyiv, after Russia said it would "punish" those responsible for deadly strikes in a part of eastern Ukraine under its control.

item.title: At least 16 people killed after a powerful bomb exploded near a railway track in southwest Pakistan, officials say
item.description: Source: AP

item.title: Taiwan, China coast guards in standoff at top of South China Sea
item.description: TAIPEI, May 24 (Reuters) - The Taiwanese and Chinese coast guards were engaged in a tense standoff for the second day near the strategically located Pratas islands at the top of the South China Sea,

item.title: Attacks on Ebola centres intensify in eastern DRC amid outbreak fears
item.description: Anger and mistrust growing over Ebola burials as traditions clash with medical response to prevent spread.

item.title: Man dies after shark attack on Australia's Great Barrier Reef
item.description: CANBERRA, May 24 (Reuters) - A 39-year-old man has died after being attacked by a shark at the Great Barrier Reef on Sunday, police said, the second fatal shark attack in Australia this month.

item.title: Dozens killed in blast targeting train in SW Pakistan: official
item.description: At least 24 people were killed on Sunday in a blast targeting a train carrying military personnel in Pakistan's turbulent southwestern province of Balochistan, a senior official said.Balochistan is Pakistan's poorest province and largest by landmass.

item.title: A powerful bomb has exploded near a railway track in southwest Pakistan, wounding more than two dozen, officials say
item.description: Source: AP

item.title: On first official India trip, Rubio tries to halt a trust deficit between Washington and Delhi
item.description: U.S. Secretary of State Marco Rubio is holding talks with his Indian counterpart Subrahmanyam Jaishankar on Sunday as the two countries look to steady ties that have fallen to their lowest point in over two decades. Rubio's visit comes during an economic and diplomatic downturn between the United States and India, strained largely by U.S. President Donald Trump's tariff policies that raised duties on several Indian exports. Rubio arrived Saturday on his first official visit to India ahead of a meeting set for Tuesday with his counterparts from India, Australia and Japan, which are members of the Indo-Pacific strategic alliance known as the Quad.

item.title: Rescuers race to find survivors after a deadly Chinese coal mine blast kills at least 82
item.description: Rescuers in northern China looked for survivors Sunday after the country's deadliest coal mine explosion in recent years killed at least 82 people. Chinese President Xi Jinping called for a thorough investigation and accountability of those responsible, after the deadly gas explosion happened Friday evening at the Liushenyu coal mine in the province of Shanxi. China’s state broadcaster CCTV earlier reported that blueprints provided by the Liushenyu coal mine did not match the actual layout, which hampered rescue efforts.

item.title: US-Iran plan extends ceasefire 60 days, reopens strait, settles nuke issue: Report
item.description: The Strait of Hormuz would reopen, and Iran would agree to clear the mines it deployed, Axios reports.

item.title: Top Asian News 6:10 a.m. GMT

item.title: Russia uses hypersonic Oreshnik missile in mass attack on Kyiv
item.description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy said Sunday Russia used the powerful hypersonic Oreshnik ballistic missile during a mass drone and missile attack on Kyiv on Sunday which killed at least two people, marking the third time the weapon has been used in the four-year war. The intense aerial assault damaged buildings across the Ukrainian capital, including near government offices, residential buildings and schools.

item.title: Lightning advance: swathes of Hanoi demolished for development
item.description: Rows of townhouses torn down in hours, roads ripped up by bulldozers and city blocks reduced to rubble in the name of progress -- giant construction sites litter Hanoi as it races ahead with urban renewal."We are unlucky to be the sufferers in this giant restructuring of Hanoi."

item.title: Heatstroke kills 16 in India as temperatures climb
item.description: At least 16 people have died of heatstroke in southern India so far this summer, officials said Sunday, as a heatwave grips swathes of the country following official health warnings.Temperatures in several cities across the South Asian country of 1.4 billion people have recently hovered well above 45C. The deaths were reported in the southern state of Telangana, with revenue minister Ponguleti Srinivasa Reddy calling for "statewide vigilance" to safeguard public health.

item.title: Iran executes person for sending information to US, Israel during war, judiciary says
item.description: DUBAI, May 24 (Reuters) - Iran executed one person for charges related to sending information to the U.S. and Israel during the war, the Iranian judiciary's Mizan news agency reported on Sunday.

item.title: China lowers death toll in Shanxi coal mine disaster to 82
item.description: BEIJING, May 24 (Reuters) - The chaos in the aftermath of China's worst mine disaster in 17 years led to a revision in the death toll to 82 killed, local officials said at a press conference late on

item.title: Inside the epicenter of the Ebola outbreak in DRC as the virus spreads
item.description: Hélène Akilimali says she’s taking every precaution against contracting Ebola, including always wearing a face mask in public. But her work as a cocoa seller in eastern Democratic Republic of Congo (DRC) - the epicenter of the latest outbreak - brings her into daily contact with other people, some of whom doubt the disease is even real, and she has no control over how they behave.

item.title: Two trapped people alive after Philippines building collapse, rescue continues
item.description: MANILA, May 24 (Reuters) - Two people feared trapped after a building collapse in the Philippines were alive and communicating with rescuers on Sunday, an official said, as rescue efforts continued.

item.title: A 9-story building under construction in Philippines collapses, possibly trapping dozens of workers
item.description: A nine-story building under construction in a city north of the Philippine capital collapsed early Sunday with 22 workers managing to get out while others remain missing, police said. The building collapsed before dawn during a thunderstorm in Angeles City in Pampanga province, north of Manila. More than 100 police and other government personnel were scrambling to rescue those believed trapped in the rubble, police Brig. Gen. Jess Mendez said.

item.title: Philippine officials say 9-story building being constructed in a northern city has collapsed, trapping dozens of workers
item.description: Source: AP

item.title: Suspect dies after trading gunfire with officers near White House, Secret Service says
item.description: By Jana Winter WASHINGTON, May 23 (Reuters) - A gunman who fired at a White House ‌checkpoint was shot by officers and died after being taken to the hospital on Saturday evening, the Secret Service

item.title: Russia launches heavy missile strikes on Kyiv after Ukraine drone attack
item.description: A large ballistic missile attack pounded Kyiv, authorities said, wounding at least five people after Moscow threatened retaliation for strikes in Russian-occupied eastern Ukraine.

item.title: In a city of high-rises, ‘cardboard grannies’ collect waste for cash
item.description: Zigzagging through bustling streets, trolleys piled high with sheets of discarded cardboard, these elderly scavengers are hard to miss in Hong Kong.

item.title: China rescuers search for missing after mine blast kills 82
item.description: Emergency rescuers in northern China were undertaking a major operation on Sunday to find two people still missing after a gas explosion at a coal mine killed at least 82.In 2023, a collapse at an open-pit coal mine in the northern Inner Mongolia region killed 53 people.

item.title: Suspect dead after approaching White House checkpoint with weapon, Secret Service says
item.description: A person who approached a White House security checkpoint and began firing at officers has died, according to federal officials. The U.S. Secret Service said in a statement late Saturday that, according to a preliminary investigation, the person approached a checkpoint shortly after 6 p.m. ET “removed a weapon from his bag and began firing at posted officers.” Officers returned fire and hit the suspect, who was transported to an area hospital, where he later died, according to the Secret Service.

item.title: California chemical tank heating up after evacuation in LA suburb, fire official says
item.description: (Adds dropped word "says" to headline) May 23 (Reuters) - Firefighters trying to prevent an explosion in a tank of hazardous chemicals in Southern California found overnight that the failing tank was

item.title: Tens of thousands rally in Serbia demanding elections
item.description: Tens of thousands of demonstrators massed in central Belgrade Saturday to renew calls for early elections that grew out of the anti-corruption movement sparked by a deadly rail station disaster.The students leading the movement hope Saturday's demonstration will relaunch their campaign to push nationalist president Vucic to call early elections.

item.title: Fact Check: Posts Saying Your Favorite Celeb Filed a $50 Million Defamation Lawsuit Against A News Host for On-Air Defamation Are NOT Real -- Spam From Vietnam
item.description: Are social media posts claiming that Pope Leo XIV and a long list of celebrities each filed a $50 million lawsuit against a news host for "vicious, calculated defamation" real? No, that's not true: None of the celebrities named in...
